title: LittlePrince_MEG_French_Read_Pallier2025
description: This dataset comprises magnetoencephalography (MEG) recordings from 50 healthy French-speaking adults reading the French text of *Le Petit Prince* presented via rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP). Each participant completed 9 experimental runs while MEG, electrooculogram, and electrocardiogram signals were continuously recorded at 1000 Hz. High-resolution anatomical MRI scans were acquired for each participant to enable source localization and cortical surface reconstruction.
methods_description: MEG data were recorded using a whole-head Elekta Neuromag TRIUX system (102 magnetometers, 204 planar gradiometers) at 1000 Hz sampling rate with online filtering (0.1–330 Hz). Words were presented individually in white font on black background for 225 ms with 50 ms inter-word intervals and 500 ms sentence-final pauses. Participants viewed stimuli from 100 cm distance while maintaining fixation. Vertical and horizontal EOG and ECG were recorded bipolarly. High-resolution T1-weighted anatomical MRI scans were acquired on a 3T Siemens Magnetom Prisma using MPRAGE sequence for coregistration and source analysis.
